L. 97–468, § 301(3)96 Stat. 2549In subsections (a) and (f), the text of 45:854(a) (2d sentence cl. (3)(A)) is omitted as executed.In subsection (a)(1), before clause (A), the text of 45:854(a) (1st sentence) is omitted as surplus because of section 24902(a) of the revised title. In clause (B)(i), the words “if the National Railroad Passenger Corporation receives notification on or before , from . . . that such State has approved” and “and if such Corporation determines that such plan is feasible” are omitted as executed. The words “rehabilitation and other . . . (including upgrading track and the signal system, ensuring safety at public and private highway and pedestrian crossings by improving signals or eliminating such crossings, and the improvement of operational portions of stations related to intercity rail passenger service)” are omitted as surplus. In clause (C), before subclause (i), the words “with respect to the main line of the Northeast Corridor” are omitted as surplus. In subclauses (i), (ii), (iv)–(viii), (xv), and (xvii), the word “Washington” is omitted as surplus. In subclause (xx), the words “at locations” are omitted as surplus.June 1, 1983In subsection (a)(2)(C), the words “passenger radio mobile telephone service on high-speed rail passenger transportation” are substituted for “high-speed rail passenger rail telephone service” for consistency in this chapter.In subsection (a)(2)(D), the word “rail” is added for consistency in the revised title.In subsection (b), the words “After the conveyance of rail properties, pursuant to section 303(b) of the Regional Rail Reorganization Act of 1973 () and ” are omitted as executed. The words “remain available to” and “the purpose of performing” are omitted as surplus.45 U.S.C. 743(b)section 851(b) of this titleIn subsection (c), the words “that portion of . . . issued by the National Railroad Passenger Corporation and” are omitted as surplus.In subsection (e)(1), the words “to be appropriated”, “undertaken or viewed as”, “entitled”, and “prepared for the United States Department of Transportation, Federal Railroad Administration, Northeast Corridor Improvement Project, in cooperation with the Federal Railroad Administration and the National Railroad Passenger Corporation (Amtrak), by Deleuw, Cather/Parsons, NECIP architect/engineer” are omitted as surplus. The words “for which amounts are authorized under” are substituted for “described in” for clarity. The words “for expenditure” are omitted as surplus.In subsection (g), the text of 45:854(a) (3d, 5th, and last sentences) is omitted as executed. The words “An amount greater than that authorized for a fiscal year” are substituted for “Funds . . . in excess of limitations imposed under the preceding sentence with respect to a fiscal year, or for fiscal years after the fiscal year ending ” to eliminate unnecessary and obsolete words. The words “under this section” are omitted as surplus. The words “amount authorized” are substituted for “limitation under such sentence” for consistency.September 30, 1983

Change of Name“William H. Gray III 30th Street Station” substituted for “30th Street Station” in subsec. (a)(1)(C)(xviii) pursuant to , set out below.section 2 of Pub. L. 113–158, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–158128 Stat. 1838Aug. 8, 2014“SECTION 1.REDESIGNATION.“The railroad station located at 2955 Market Street in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, commonly known as ‘30th Street Station’, shall be known and designated as the ‘William H. Gray III 30th Street Station’.“SEC. 2.REFERENCES.“Any reference in a law, map, regulation, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the railroad station referred to in section 1 shall be deemed to be a reference to the ‘William H. Gray III 30th Street Station’.”

children.label: A
children.text: at least $27,000,000 for equipment modification and replacement that a State or a local or regional transportation authority must bear because of the electrification conversion system of the Northeast Corridor under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: to improve the main line track between the Northeast Corridor main line and Atlantic City, New Jersey, to ensure that the track, consistent with a plan New Jersey developed in consultation with Amtrak to provide rail passenger transportation between the Northeast Corridor main line and Atlantic City, New Jersey, would be of sufficient quality to allow safe rail passenger transportation at a minimum of 79 miles an hour not later than ; andSeptember 30, 1985

children.label: ii
children.text: to promote rail passenger use of the track.

children.label: i
children.text: develop Union Station in the District of Columbia;

children.label: ii
children.text: install 189 track-miles, and renew 133 track-miles, of concrete ties with continuously welded rail between the District of Columbia and New York, New York;

children.label: iii
children.text: install reverse signaling between Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, and Morrisville, Pennsylvania, on numbers 2 and 3 track;

children.label: iv
children.text: restore ditch drainage in concrete tie locations between the District of Columbia and New York, New York;

children.label: v
children.text: undercut 83 track-miles between the District of Columbia and New York, New York;

children.label: vi
children.text: rehabilitate bridges between the District of Columbia and New York, New York (including Hi line);

children.label: vii
children.text: develop a maintenance of way equipment repair facility between the District of Columbia and New York, New York, and build maintenance of way bases at Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, Sunnyside, New York, and Cedar Hill, Connecticut;

children.label: viii
children.text: stabilize the roadbed between the District of Columbia and New York, New York;

children.label: ix
children.text: automate the Bush River Drawbridge at milepost 72.14;

children.label: x
children.text: improve the New York Service Facility to develop rolling stock repair capability;

children.label: xi
children.text: install a rail car washer facility at Philadelphia, Pennsylvania;

children.label: xii
children.text: restore storage tracks and buildings at the Washington Service Facility;

children.label: xiii
children.text: install centralized traffic control from Landlith, Delaware, to Philadelphia, Pennsylvania;

children.label: xiv
children.text: improve track, including high speed surfacing, ballast cleaning, and associated equipment repair and material distribution;

children.label: xv
children.text: rehabilitate interlockings between the District of Columbia and New York, New York;

children.label: xvi
children.text: paint the Connecticut River, Groton, and Pelham Bay bridges;

children.label: xvii
children.text: provide additional catenary renewal and power supply upgrading between the District of Columbia and New York, New York;

children.label: xviii
children.text: rehabilitate structural, electrical, and mechanical systems at the William H. Gray III 30th Street Station in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania;

children.label: xix
children.text: install evacuation and fire protection facilities in tunnels in New York, New York;

children.label: xx
children.text: improve the communication and signal systems between Wilmington, Delaware, and Boston, Massachusetts, on the Northeast Corridor main line, and between Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, and Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, on the Harrisburg Line;

children.label: xxi
children.text: improve the electric traction systems between Wilmington, Delaware, and Newark, New Jersey;

children.label: xxii
children.text: install baggage rack restraints, seat back guards, and seat lock devices on 348 passenger cars operating in the Northeast Corridor;

children.label: xxiii
children.text: install 44 event recorders and 10 electronic warning devices on locomotives operating within the Northeast Corridor; and

children.label: xxiv
children.text: acquire cab signal test boxes and install 9 wayside loop code transmitters for use within the Northeast Corridor.

children.label: A
children.text: not more than $150,000,000 to achieve the goal of section 24902(a)(3) of this title.1

children.label: B
children.text: not more than $120,000,000 to acquire interests in property in the Northeast Corridor.

children.label: C
children.text: not more than $650,000 to develop and use mobile radio frequencies for passenger radio mobile telephone service on high-speed rail passenger transportation.

children.label: D
children.text: not more than $20,000,000 to acquire and improve interests in rail property designated under section 206(c)(1)(D) of the Regional Rail Reorganization Act of 1973 ().45 U.S.C. 716(c)(1)(D)

children.label: E
children.text: not more than $37,000,000 to carry out section 24902(a)(7) and (j) of this title.1

children.heading: .—Emergency Maintenance
children.label: b .—Emergency Maintenance
children.text: Not more than $25,000,000 of the amount appropriated under the Act of (, ), may be used by Amtrak for emergency maintenance on rail property designated under section 206(c)(1)(C) of the Regional Rail Reorganization Act of 1973 ().February 28, 1975Public Law 94–689 Stat. 1145 U.S.C. 716(c)(1)(C)

children.heading: .—Priority in Using Certain Amounts
children.label: c .—Priority in Using Certain Amounts
children.text: Amounts appropriated under subsection (a)(2)(B) and (D) of this section shall be used first to repay, with interest, obligations guaranteed under section 602 of the Rail Passenger Service Act, if the proceeds of those obligations were used to pay the expenses of acquiring interests in property referred to in subsection (a)(2)(B) and (D).

children.heading: .—Prohibition on Subsidizing Commuter and Freight Operating Losses
children.label: d .—Prohibition on Subsidizing Commuter and Freight Operating Losses
children.text: Amounts appropriated under this section may not be used to subsidize operating losses of commuter rail or rail freight transportation.

A project for which amounts are authorized under subsection (a)(1)(C) of this section is a part of the Northeast Corridor improvement program and is not a substitute for improvements specified in the document “Corridor Master Plan II, NECIP Restructured Program” of January, 1982. However, Amtrak may defer the project to carry out the improvement and rehabilitation for which amounts are authorized under subsection (a)(1)(B) of this section. The total cost of the project that Amtrak defers may not be substantially more than the amount Amtrak is required to expend or reserve under subsection (a)(1)(B).
is deemed not to be fulfilled until the projects under subsection (a)(1)(C) of this section are completed.Section 24902 of this title

children.heading: .—Availability of Amounts
children.label: f .—Availability of Amounts
children.text: Amounts appropriated under subsection (a)(1) and (2)(A) and (C)–(E) of this section remain available until expended.

children.heading: .—Authorizations Increased by Prior Year Deficiencies
children.label: g .—Authorizations Increased by Prior Year Deficiencies
children.text: An amount greater than that authorized for a fiscal year may be appropriated to the extent that the amount appropriated for any prior fiscal year is less than the amount authorized for that year.
